Golești](/wiki/Schitu_Gole%C8%99ti "Schitu Golești") * [Slobozia](/wiki/Slobozia,_Arg

(Romanian pronunciation: [[ʃtefəˈneʃtʲ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Romanian "Help:IPA/Romanian")) is a town in [Argeș County](/wiki/Arge%C8%99_County "Argeș County"), [Muntenia](/wiki/Muntenia "Muntenia"), [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania"). The town administers seven villages: Enculești, Golești, Izvorani, Ștefăneștii Noi, Valea Mare-Podgoria, Viișoara, and Zăvoi. At the [2021 census](/wiki/2021_Romanian_census "2021 Romanian census"), the town had a population of 15,931.
["2021 Romanian census"](https://www.recensamantromania.ro/rezultate-rpl-2021/rezultate-definitive-caracteristici-demografice/). National Institute of Statistics. 1 December 2021.
["Results of the 2024 local elections"](https://prezenta.roaep.ro/locale09062024v2/). Central Electoral Bureau. Retrieved 17 December 2025.

**Ștefănești** (Romanian pronunciation: [[ʃtefəˈneʃtʲ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Romanian "Help:IPA/Romanian")) is a town in [Argeș County](/wiki/Arge%C8%99_County "Argeș County"), [Muntenia](/wiki/Muntenia "Muntenia"), [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania"). The town administers seven villages: Enculești, Golești, Izvorani, Ștefăneștii Noi, Valea Mare-Podgoria, Viișoara, and Zăvoi. At the [2021 census](/wiki/2021_Romanian_census "2021 Romanian census"), the town had a population of 15,931.

The town is located in the central part of Argeș County, just east of the county seat, [Pitești](/wiki/Pite%C8%99ti "Pitești"). It lies on the banks of [Râul Doamnei](/wiki/R%C3%A2ul_Doamnei "Râul Doamnei") and its tributary, the [Valea Mare](/wiki/Valea_Mare_(R%C3%A2ul_Doamnei) "Valea Mare (Râul Doamnei)"). It is located in one of the best-known [wine-producing](/wiki/Romanian_wine "Romanian wine") regions of Muntenia.

## Notable persons

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=%C8%98tef%C4%83ne%C8%99ti,_Arge%C8%99&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Notable persons")]

* [Dinu Brătianu](/wiki/Dinu_Br%C4%83tianu "Dinu Brătianu") (1866–1950), engineer and politician who led the National Liberal Party (PNL) from 1934 to 1947
* [Ion I. C. Brătianu](/wiki/Ion_I._C._Br%C4%83tianu "Ion I. C. Brătianu") (1864–1927), politician, leader of the PNL, Prime Minister of Romania for five terms, and Foreign Minister on several occasions
* [Vintilă Brătianu](/wiki/Vintil%C4%83_Br%C4%83tianu "Vintilă Brătianu") (1867–1930), politician who served as Prime Minister of Romania in 1927–28
* [Dănuț Coman](/wiki/D%C4%83nu%C8%9B_Coman "Dănuț Coman") (born 1979), footballer
* [Dinicu Golescu](/wiki/Dinicu_Golescu "Dinicu Golescu") (1777–1830), Wallachian man of letters, mostly noted for his travel writings and journalism
* [Leonard Manole](/wiki/Leonard_Manole "Leonard Manole") (born 1993), footballer
* [Ionuț Moșteanu](/wiki/Ionu%C8%9B_Mo%C8%99teanu_(politician) "Ionuț Moșteanu (politician)") (born 1973), politician
* [Florentin Nicolae](/wiki/Florentin_Nicolae "Florentin Nicolae") (born 1981), alpine skier
* [George Olteanu](/wiki/George_Olteanu "George Olteanu") (born 1974), boxer
